## What the existing draft covers

Inventor: Xinhan Tong · Address: 405 S Fernwood Cir, Sunnyvale, CA 94085
Title: *Agent Runtime Operating System with Hierarchical Execution Loops,
Boundary-Based Security Model, Self-Evolving Meta-Cognition Engine, and Token-Budget
Resource Management for Autonomous AI Agent Orchestration*

Coverage against Nirmana's flagged innovations (from night-runner-projects.md §7):

| Innovation | Section | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Three-Zone permission model (Green/Yellow/Red) | §4.8, Claim 2 | covered |
| Loop 0 meta-cognition / self-evolution | §4.5, Claim 3 | covered |
| Ralph Loop / fresh-context iteration (Loop 1) | §4.3, Claim 1 | covered |
| Hardware-aware scheduling (TAPAS) | §4.13, Claim 4 | covered |

Plus 15 additional sections not flagged by Nirmana but already drafted:
Constellation model, three-loop hierarchy, cognitive memory hierarchy, four-layer
prompt-injection defense, Night Runner unattended execution, cryptographically
signed evolution chain, memory-bound agent identity, UEI, GILD error distillation,
dynamic skill library, immutable audit trail, method claims (13–17), Beauregard
CRM claims (18–19), dependent claims (20–24), hardened/reworked claims,
progress.txt data format spec, shadow-testing replay mechanism, Nirmanakaya user
detection, task complexity classifier.

Claims: 24 total. Alice/Mayo §101 analysis included. 13 drawings listed
(ASCII-diagrammed in spec; formal drawings flagged as TODO for non-provisional).

## Why nothing new was drafted

1. **Draft is current.** Last modification 2026-03-24. No aros-kernel commits since
   2026-03-22 (patent draft post-dates all kernel work).
2. **All Nirmana-flagged innovations covered.** Spot-check of §4.8 (Three-Zone) and
   §4.13 (hardware-aware) confirms substantive, enabled descriptions with concrete
   mechanisms (seccomp/App Sandbox/Job Objects; eBPF/RAPL/NVML sensor integration).
3. **Patent_Additions_Draft already merged** into main application (lines 1202–1526).
   Kept as separate file for traceability.

## Open questions for Eddie (on `/back`)

1. **Filing decision.** Draft is ready. Does Eddie want to:
   - (a) File self-pro-se via USPTO EFS-Web (~$75 micro-entity / $150 small-entity)
   - (b) Engage patent counsel for review-then-file
   - (c) Hold — keep as trade secret / defer filing
2. **Inventor info.** Address in draft is 405 S Fernwood Cir, Sunnyvale, CA 94085 —
   confirm still current.
3. **Filing date in header** lists "Mar/23, 2026" — this is a planned date, not
   actual. Needs update to real filing date before submission.
4. **Formal drawings.** 13 figures are described in ASCII; USPTO accepts these for
   provisional but non-provisional will need formal drawings. Defer to conversion.
5. **Public disclosure audit.** AROS deep-research reports, ArXiv paper v2.3
   (committed 2026-03-24 in aros-design), and GitHub repos may constitute prior
   public disclosure that starts a 1-year US filing clock (absolute bar in most
   foreign jurisdictions). Counsel should assess before any non-provisional/PCT.
6. **Continuation strategy.** Draft recommends three continuation patents
   (Centurion multi-agent, token-cost economic model, Memory-as-a-Service) — review
   whether to carve those out of provisional or fold in.

## Addendum — Phase 1 kernel work post-dating the draft (FLAGGED GAP)

**Update after rebasing local:** origin/main has 13 commits dated 2026-04-06, all
landed AFTER the patent draft was written (2026-03-24). Six new Rust modules exist
that may contain patentable subject matter not covered by the current draft:

| Module | Files | Possible novelty vs existing claims |
|---|---|---|
| `src/supervisor/` | process, kernel, handler, health | Erlang-style supervisor tree for agent lifecycle — not in current claims |
| `src/store/` | engine (SQLite/WAL), acl | ACL-enforced state store; Claim 3 covers metric-store read-only perms but not general state ACL |
| `src/dispatch/` | rpc, server, contracts | JSON-RPC "loop trigger contracts" for external loop invocation — not in claims |
| `src/governor/` | admission, budget, governor | Consolidated resource governor; extends Claim 4 token budget to unified admission |
| `src/adapter/` | circuit_breaker, provider, resolver | Model adapter sidecar with circuit breaker; extends Claim 4 cost-aware router |
| `src/envelope/` | task_envelope | Serializable task envelope; potentially a concrete realization of Claim 12 UEI |
| commit `8201895` | (scheduler integration) | Persisting SIE drift score + policy head on MetaCycleComplete — new feedback signal beyond §4.5's seven channels |

**Recommendation for Eddie:** Before filing provisional, decide whether to:
- (a) File current draft as-is; cover new modules via continuation patent or CIP
- (b) Supplement current draft with a §4.20 "Phase 1 Realization Architecture" section
      enumerating these mechanisms, then file
- (c) Have counsel review the Phase 1 code and advise on claim scope

Note: once the provisional is filed, only material disclosed in it gets the priority
date. New matter in a later non-provisional or CIP gets a later date. Erring toward
over-disclosure in the provisional is the conservative move.
